title: IGF-1 DES
description: IGF-1 DES is a truncated variant of Insulin-like Growth Factor 1 that lacks the first three amino acids at the N-terminus, significantly reducing its binding to IGF-binding proteins. This modification makes it approximately 10 times more potent than standard IGF-1, allowing for rapid stimulation of cellular hypertrophy and proliferation through localized receptor activation.

research.title: Des(1-3)IGF-I: a truncated form of insulin-like growth factor-I
research.summary: This variant is 10-fold more potent than standard IGF-1 due to reduced binding with inhibitory proteins, showing significant anabolic effects in vivo.

research.title: Secretion of insulin-like growth factor II (IGF-II) and IGF-binding protein-2 by intestinal epithelial (IEC-6) cells
research.summary: Research demonstrates that IGF-1 variants are significantly more potent than insulin or IGF-II in stimulating protein synthesis and DNA replication in epithelial cells.

research.title: Effects of IGF-1 on the Cardiovascular System
research.summary: IGF-1 acts as a powerful anabolic hormone that regulates cell survival, metabolic homeostasis, and vascular relaxation through nitric oxide pathways.

sentiment.summary: Users generally report high satisfaction with IGF-1 DES for its rapid effects on muscle fullness and injury recovery, though it is viewed as a specialized tool for experienced biohackers.

effects.label: Binding Affinity:
effects.description: Highly effective because it bypasses IGF-binding proteins (IGFBPs) that usually inhibit the activity of standard IGF-1.

effects.label: Site Injection:
effects.description: Most effective when administered locally to muscle groups immediately following training.

effects.label: Gut Health:
effects.description: Strong evidence suggests selective anabolic effects on intestinal tissues, which may aid in inflammatory bowel conditions.

effects.label: Potency Comparison:
effects.description: Notably more effective at lower doses than regular IGF-1 or IGF-1 LR3 for immediate cellular response.

dosage.label: Targeted Dosing:
dosage.description: Commonly used at 20-50mcg per day, often split across bilateral muscle groups.

dosage.label: Timing:
dosage.description: Most users recommend administration 5-20 minutes post-workout to take advantage of sensitized receptors.

dosage.label: Reconstitution:
dosage.description: Must be reconstituted with bacteriostatic water or acetic acid and stored at cool temperatures to maintain stability.

dosage.label: Cycling:
dosage.description: Often used for short bursts (4-6 weeks) rather than long-term administration due to its high potency.

sideEffects.label: Hypoglycemia:
sideEffects.description: Users report potential drops in blood sugar levels, necessitating a post-administration carbohydrate source.

sideEffects.label: Local Inflammation:
sideEffects.description: Temporary redness or swelling at the site of injection is common.

sideEffects.label: Organ Growth:
sideEffects.description: Concerns regarding potential growth of unintended tissues (like the gut) if used excessively long-term.

sideEffects.label: Cancer Risk:
sideEffects.description: High IGF-1 levels are theoretically linked to accelerated growth of pre-existing malignant cells.

availability.label: Research Status:
availability.description: Primarily sold through research chemical sources; not approved for human medical use in several jurisdictions.

availability.label: Sourcing Difficulty:
availability.description: High prevalence of low-quality or mislabeled product in the gray market; lab testing and verification are frequently discussed on Reddit.
